hard.next_step: Jun 29, 2026 Received documents for RFP round 2, due 17.7. Second round of RFP to start by end of June; implementation partners contacted in parallel; call Thomas Kirmse on 25.6.
hard.ais_why: AI-rich scope with three AI modules (ANA, Merlin, Merlin Intake) alongside iContract/iSource/iSupplier/eInvoicing/iRisk at $1.2M; buyer narrative centred on platform consolidation and automation rather than AI-as-selection-criterion.
hard.competitor: Undisclosed finalist(s) in Bosch's 4-5 vendor tender; status quo / fragmented best-of-breed stack
hard.primary_competitor: Undisclosed finalist(s) in Bosch's 4-5 vendor tender

north_star_verdict.headline: Genuinely healthy, deeply engaged deal: a two-week on-site POC closed 22 May with strong feedback and Bosch is entering commercials, but the 31 July close cannot survive RFP round 2 (documents received 29 Jun, response due 17 Jul), implementation-partner selection, pricing, economic-buyer approval and contracting.
north_star_verdict.math: Shortlisted since 2026-04-30 (about 67 days in stage); close 2026-07-31 is 25 days out as of 2026-07-06. POC ran 11-22 May and closed positively. RFP round 2 documents received 29 Jun with response due 17 Jul, leaving only two weeks before the forecast close for partner selection, commercial/pricing (promised for June, just starting), economic-buyer approval (buyer never engaged) and MSA/SOW plus InfoSec (blocked on written BYOK/SCIM commitment). On the buyer's own stated timeline award was targeted for July, project start August 2026 and go-live Q4 2027, so the 31 July close realistically slips 60-90 days into Q4 2026.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Best Case is not defensible for a 31 July close; move the date to Q4 2026 (Oct/Nov) since RFP round 2 alone runs to 17 Jul before pricing, economic-buyer approval and contracting can begin.
evidence: Two-week on-site POC ran 11-22 May 2026 across S2C, P2O and technical tracks (POC kickoff, 11 May 2026).

Closing call 22 May 2026: the buyer delivered detailed positive findings ('your platform provides a very solid foundation'); Thomas Kirmse stated Bosch is 'in the contract discussions and commercial discussion' and will 'step into further discussions in June'.
Technical deep dive 26 May 2026: Bruno Streitenberger said 'based on this meeting, we will reevaluate our evaluation' after API-documentation questions went unmet for three weeks.
Next Step (Salesforce): RFP round 2 documents received 29 Jun 2026, response due 17 Jul; implementation partners being contacted in parallel; call Thomas Kirmse 25 Jun.
Buyer email received 2026-06-02 confirms two-way engagement within the last month (deal pulse: live).

metrics.narrative: Bosch articulated clear scale and automation drivers verbatim in Customer_Business_Problem__c: highly fragmented systems (~40 ERPs) and heterogeneous indirect-procurement processes lacking efficiency, automation, data consistency, supplier-risk transparency and user experience, with a stated goal of one unified, modern, scalable S2P platform ('Entire S2P project global'). During the 22 May POC closing the buyer confirmed strong functional fit ('overall the fit is very good') across S2C, P2O, demand and supplier management. What is still missing is a Bosch-owned, euro-denominated savings or ROI case agreed with their finance lead; the value language remains Zycus's, not the buyer's. Building that quantified business case with Bosch finance stays a ranked move.

economic_buyer.narrative: After roughly eight months, a $1.2M deal and a two-week on-site POC, the budget owner has never appeared on any recorded call, which is the single biggest structural risk at this stage. Every engaged Bosch stakeholder is operational (Category Purchasing and IT-process): Thomas Kirmse owns the commercial thread but is thinly present on calls, and on 12 May the Next Step note 'John suggested to engage with c level once we are through the current stage of testing' shows C-level contact was consciously deferred, with no C-level engagement since the POC ended 22 May. Thomas Kirmse (Category Purchasing Software & IT Services) is the confirmed route to power and controls the commercial and partner discussion, so identifying and engaging the budget owner through him is the priority move now that testing has concluded.
sources: Avoma call manifest 19 Feb-26 May 2026 (EB absent)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are explicit from the 22 May POC closing findings: a functional end-to-end sourcing process, seamless sourcing-to-contracting, handling of complex Bosch sourcing (auctions, non-tender allocation to multiple suppliers), integrated and automated AI across process steps, ESG / supplier-risk steering end-to-end, and enterprise integration into SAP S/4HANA. Bosch marked several as partially met out of the box ('not all functionalities currently meet our expectations 100%', 'seamless sourcing-to-contracting not fully seamless'), and named hard technical criteria as critical deviations: SCIM identity management, bring-your-own-key (BYOK) encryption, API documentation/versioning and push-event support. These are confirmed, buyer-stated criteria, and the RFP round 2 response due 17 Jul must be shaped directly to them.

decision_process.narrative: The call sequence reveals the process the buyer is running: a structured multi-vendor tender (long-list of 4-5 suppliers per Next Step 2 Feb 2026) that ran a two-week on-site POC 11-22 May, followed by a formal written findings review, and on 22 May Thomas Kirmse stated Bosch will 'first close the PoC with all market participants' and then 'define the next strategy' before entering commercials. As of 29 Jun the process moved into RFP round 2 (response due 17 Jul) with implementation-partner selection running in parallel. The visible approval chain remains procurement-led (Kirmse for commercials, Nordmann as project lead) with C-level approval deferred; the formal budget-approval and sign-off path above procurement is not yet mapped.
sources: Salesforce Next Step (2 Feb, 22 Jun, 29 Jun 2026)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are only partly visible. Thomas Kirmse confirmed on 22 May that Bosch will close the POC with all participants and then enter commercial and contract discussions expected in June; Dirk Fischbach committed the implementation-partner matrix, which was sent to Thomas on 22 May (EY previously ruled out). A Cloud SaaS security questionnaire (2 Apr) and a Corporate Social Responsibility RFI (rfi_23740, Apr-May) were issued during the POC, signalling formal procurement and InfoSec gating ahead, and Zycus owes written SCIM and BYOK commitments that Bosch has flagged as a mandate. No MSA / SOW, legal review or signature authority has been discussed yet and a system integrator is expected to join for implementation. A mutual close plan naming pricing, security-review, legal and partner steps against dated owners would create the missing visibility.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is specific and buyer-confirmed: Bosch operates highly fragmented systems (~40 ERPs) and heterogeneous indirect-procurement processes with poor efficiency, automation, data consistency, supplier-risk transparency and user experience, and wants one unified, modern, scalable S2P platform (Customer_Business_Problem__c). During the POC Bosch probed exactly these pains: complex sourcing (auctions, non-tender part allocation), seamless sourcing-to-contracting, ESG / supplier-risk steering, and multi-system SAP integration across split company codes, and Rebecca Elias's 22 May findings confirmed both the fit and the residual out-of-the-box gaps. The pain owners are the operational procurement and IT-process teams (Nordmann Sabina, Bruno Streitenberger, and the S2C solution managers) who will become the platform's power admins.

champion.narrative: Operational advocacy is broad and genuine but has not reached power level. Thomas Kirmse (Category Purchasing Software & IT Services) is the named champion candidate and commercial point of contact, is 'open for communications', and the Next Step shows a scheduled call with him on 25 Jun, yet he appears on few calls and his internal advocacy to the budget owner is not yet evidenced. Nordmann Sabina coordinated the entire on-site POC as project lead and Rebecca Elias delivered the structured, constructive closing assessment, both giving Zycus deep access and positive feedback. What is still missing is a champion who is actively selling Zycus to the budget owner against the other tender finalists; converting Kirmse (or recruiting an exec sponsor) into that internal seller is the priority.

competition.narrative: Bosch is running a structured 4-5 vendor tender: Next Step (2 Feb 2026) records 'we are in a long-list of 4-5 suppliers' with award targeted for July, and on 22 May Thomas Kirmse confirmed Bosch will 'close the PoC with all market participants' before deciding, so multiple rivals remain live. No competitor has been named by Bosch on any of the ~28 touchpoints, so the specific rival logos are a genuine knowledge gap; the only explicitly characterised alternative is the status-quo fragmented best-of-breed stack the platform would replace. Zycus's wedge is its organically-built agentic AI (Merlin) and proven live SAP S/4HANA integration via the Rojo adapters, both surfaced in the POC; the risk is an unnamed rival building the economic-buyer relationship first, so uncovering the named shortlist from Kirmse is a ranked move.

critical_signals.text: The 26 May integration deep dive left Bruno Streitenberger saying Bosch will reevaluate after three weeks of unmet API-documentation requests, the live objection that could cost the down-select.
Bosch is still choosing among 4-5 shortlisted vendors and will finish the POC with all participants before awarding in July, so the field is live and the finalists are undisclosed.
Thomas Kirmse opened commercial and contract discussions on 22 May and Bosch is now in RFP round 2 with a response due 17 Jul, but pricing has only just started and no budget owner is engaged.
Bosch named SCIM identity management and bring-your-own-key encryption as mandates on 22 May, and Zycus owes the written commitment it promised but has not yet delivered.
No economic buyer has ever joined a call after eight months and a $1.2M POC; C-level contact was deliberately deferred until testing concluded, which it now has.

deal_movement.summary: Live and progressing: the on-site POC closed positively on 22 May, Bosch moved into RFP round 2 (documents received 29 Jun, response due 17 Jul) with implementation-partner selection in parallel, and the last two-way buyer touch was an email on 2 Jun.

items.change: Two-week on-site POC completed across S2C, P2O and technical tracks
Buyer delivered detailed positive POC findings and confirmed entry into commercial and contract discussions
Bruno Streitenberger said Bosch will reevaluate after API-documentation gaps in the integration deep dive
Amount raised from $300k to $1.2M and stage moved Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted
RFP round 2 documents received, response due 17 Jul; implementation partners being contacted

competitive_position.summary: Bosch is running a live 4-5 vendor tender with a July award targeted and will 'close the PoC with all market participants' before deciding, so the strongest current threat is the undisclosed finalist field in the active down-select, not any status-quo option. No rival is named on any call or in Salesforce, which remains a genuine knowledge gap and the priority to close with Thomas Kirmse. The clearest characterised alternative is the status quo of fragmented best-of-breed tools the platform would replace. Zycus's differentiators are organically-built agentic AI (Merlin) and proven live SAP S/4HANA integration, both praised in the POC.

competitors.how_we_win: Position the unified 12-product S2P suite as single-platform simplification versus tool sprawl, and de-risk the customization load Bosch flagged with a clear implementation and partner plan so status-quo comfort does not win by default.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong functional fit confirmed across all modules, with residual gaps on out-of-the-box maturity, complex-sourcing usability, integration transparency and specific security features.

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers matter most now: convert Kirmse into a power-level champion, clear the live integration objection, and force the economic buyer into the room before the July award.
flags: Convert Thomas Kirmse from commercial contact into an active champion selling Zycus to the budget owner before the July award.
Close the API-documentation and SCIM/BYOK gaps fast: Bruno Streitenberger's 26 May 'we will reevaluate' is the live technical objection that could cost the down-select.
Force the economic buyer into the room now that the POC has concluded; a $1.2M award with no budget-owner contact after eight months is the top structural gap.

champion_strength.summary: Engagement is broad and operationally strong (Nordmann coordinated the POC, Elias delivered positive findings, Kirmse owns commercials), but there is no confirmed champion actively selling Zycus to the economic buyer. Thomas Kirmse is the designated candidate and route to power yet is thinly present on calls, so champion strength is developing: deep operational advocacy exists, power-level advocacy does not.
champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole (Thomas Kirmse); Avoma POC closing 22 May 2026; Salesforce Next Step (22 Jun 2026)

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS is 6/Moderate on a genuinely AI-rich scope (ANA, Merlin, Merlin Intake). Bosch ran a dedicated AI deep-dive on 21 May and praised the agentic-AI potential ('the AI and automation concepts, the agents, show a very cool potential'), but flagged current AI as basic and not fully integrated end-to-end, so the story is landing only partially.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 6 / Moderate reads accurately against the calls: Bosch is interested in agentic AI and ran a specific AI deep-dive, but judged the delivered AI features as basic and wanted the roadmap, so appetite is real but not yet a decisive selection criterion.

items.detail: Budget owner / economic buyer has never appeared on a call after eight months and a $1.2M POC; C-level engagement was deliberately deferred.
Bruno Streitenberger said Bosch will 'reevaluate' after unmet API-documentation and push-event questions; SCIM and BYOK flagged as mandates and not yet committed in writing.
31 July close is not credible: RFP round 2 response alone is due 17 Jul before pricing, EB approval and contracting; buyer's own award target is July with project start August.
Live multi-vendor tender: Bosch will close the POC with all 4-5 participants before awarding, and the rival finalists remain undisclosed.
Bosch repeatedly flagged heavy customization and configuration effort and out-of-the-box gaps for its complex sourcing, which could feed a status-quo or phased-implementation preference.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and two-way (POC closed positively 22 May, RFP round 2 underway, buyer email 2 Jun), but momentum above the operational layer is thin: no economic buyer, an open technical objection, and pricing only just starting.
items: Positive POC findings and explicit intent to enter commercials (22 May 2026).
RFP round 2 documents received 29 Jun with a buyer-set 17 Jul deadline.
Bruno Streitenberger's 'we will reevaluate' remains an unresolved technical risk (26 May 2026).

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Bosch just closed a strong two-week on-site POC and is racing into RFP round 2 (response due 17 Jul), but it is still a live 4-5 vendor tender with no economic buyer in the room and an open integration objection, so the win rides on converting Kirmse to power and clearing the technical gaps before the July award.

win_position.text: Shortlisted caps confidence near 70; this earns roughly 63 on strong POC fit ('overall the fit is very good', 22 May) and broad operational engagement, held below the cap because no economic buyer has ever engaged and the field is a live 4-5 vendor tender.
Zycus differentiates on organically-built agentic AI (Merlin) and proven live SAP S/4HANA integration via Rojo adapters, both demonstrated and praised across the POC and integration deep-dives.
The budget owner has never appeared on a call after eight months and a $1.2M POC, and C-level contact was deliberately deferred, so we have no confirmed route to sign-off (Next Step, 12 May).
Bruno Streitenberger said Bosch will 'reevaluate' after API-documentation, SCIM and BYOK gaps went unmet for three weeks, a live technical objection that could cost the down-select (26 May).

deal_momentum.text: Bosch moved from POC closure straight into RFP round 2 with a buyer-set 17 Jul deadline and implementation-partner selection running in parallel (Next Step, 29 Jun).
The buyer named concrete forward dates on 22 May (award July, project start August 2026, go-live Q4 2027) and confirmed entry into commercial and contract discussions.
Commercials and pricing have only just started and were promised for June, so the commercial thread lags the 31 July forecast close.

customer_commitment.text: Bosch invested a two-week on-site POC and a dedicated AI deep-dive and delivered detailed written findings, signalling serious evaluation commitment (22 May).
Commitment is entirely operational: Thomas Kirmse owns commercials but is thinly present on calls and no budget owner has sponsored the deal, so there is no power-level commitment yet.

deal_risk.text: The 31 July close is not credible: RFP round 2 alone runs to 17 Jul before pricing, EB approval and contracting, so the date realistically slips into Q4 2026.
It remains an active multi-vendor tender with undisclosed finalists, and an unnamed rival could reach the economic buyer first while Zycus is still operational-only.

executive_alignment.evidence: Economic buyer never engaged after eight months and a $1.2M POC; C-level contact deliberately deferred (Next Step, 12 May 2026).

timeline.evidence: 31 July close cannot survive RFP round 2 (due 17 Jul) plus pricing, EB approval and contracting; buyer's own award target is July with project start August.

scope_change.to: $1.2M full S2P suite (12 products) in Shortlisted
scope_change.detail: On 30 Apr 2026 the amount was raised from $300k to $1.2M and the stage moved Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted as the deal broadened to the full global S2P suite; scope is expanding, not narrowing.

customer_preference.evidence: Bosch praised the platform as 'a very solid foundation' with 'overall fit is very good' and gave deep POC access, but is still choosing among 4-5 vendors and flagged out-of-the-box gaps and 'we will reevaluate' on integration, so positive but not decisive.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain and scale drivers are clear (~40 ERPs, consolidation, automation, ~+50% users / +100% transactions per prior MEDDPICC) but no euro-denominated ROI or savings case has been agreed with Bosch finance.
